title: Justified .unwrap() calls have SAFETY comments not documenting panics
description:
Several .unwrap() calls are justified with SAFETY comments explaining why they cannot fail, but SAFETY is semantically for unsafe code. Consider using a different comment prefix (e.g., `// Infallible:` or `// PANIC: unreachable because...`) for documentation clarity:

- lichen-link/src/schnorr.rs:25,54 — `// SAFETY: hash is 64 bytes, so...`
- lichen-link/src/identity.rs:18 — `// SAFETY: SHA-256 output is 32 bytes...`
- lichen-link/src/schnorr.rs:84,161 — `// SAFETY: sig is exactly 48 bytes...`
- lichen-rpl/src/message.rs:88,152,321 — `// SAFETY: length check above ensures...`

These are not unsafe code blocks; they are infallibility arguments. Using SAFETY for non-unsafe code may confuse reviewers looking for actual unsafe blocks.
close_reason: Changed SAFETY to INFALLIBLE for unwrap justifications in identity.rs, schnorr.rs, and message.rs
